behavior_in_task_description.reason: Every test assertion references file paths (shape_state.ex, stream.ex), function names (check_fast_loop, clear_fast_loop), literal strings (@fast_loop_window_ms 500, @fast_loop_threshold 5, @fast_loop_max_count 5, recent_requests: [], fast_loop_consecutive_count: 0, check_fast_loop(stream), ShapeState.clear_fast_loop), spec strings (@spec check_fast_loop(t())), and test file paths (shape_state_test.exs) — all of which are explicitly stated in instruction.md.

no_solution_leakage.reason: The instruction specifies exact implementation details: exact function names, exact spec signatures, exact struct field names with default values, exact module attribute names and values, exact call sites in stream.ex (check_fast_loop(stream), ShapeState.clear_fast_loop). This goes far beyond describing what's broken — it prescribes the precise code structure, leaving almost no design decisions for the agent.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests like test_fast_loop_check_exists assert exact string literals like '@spec check_fast_loop(t())' and 'def check_fast_loop' via text search. A correct alternative that used a different function name (e.g., detect_fast_loop), different spec format, or different internal organization would fail. However, since the instruction dictates these exact names, alternative correct implementations are effectively excluded by design — but this means the tests assert on gold-specific identifiers rather t

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests test_fast_loop_check_exists, test_fast_loop_constants, test_fast_loop_struct_fields, test_clear_fast_loop_exists, test_stream_calls_check_fast_loop, and test_stream_clears_fast_loop_on_live are all purely string-matching via 'assert X in content' with no code execution. Only test_elixir_tests_pass and the compile/format/unit test functions actually execute code. The majority of f2p tests (6 out of ~7 novel tests) are text-only.

test_not_tautological.reason: test_elixir_tests_pass runs 'mix test' on shape_state_test.exs and requires 8 passing tests plus returncode 0. A stub implementation that just adds the string literals without real logic would fail those 8 unit tests. The compile-with-warnings test also provides a behavioral check. So the overall suite cannot be passed by a trivial stub.

anti_cheating_measures.reason: The solution is fetched via 'curl -sL https://github.com/electric-sql/electric/pull/4028.patch' in solve.sh, which is accessible from within the container. An agent could read /solution/solve.sh (or the solve.sh file if accessible), extract the PR URL, and apply the patch directly. Additionally, the test file at test/electric/client/shape_state_test.exs already exists in the container (placed by the gold solution setup or pre-existing), so the agent could inspect it to reverse-engineer the exact

behavior_in_tests.reason: Instruction requires: (1) exponential backoff on subsequent detections, (2) raising Client.Error after 5 consecutive detections, (3) sliding window of 500ms with 5+ requests triggering detection, (4) clearing shape handle on first detection. The Python test harness doesn't directly test any of these behavioral requirements — it only checks string presence and delegates to the hidden shape_state_test.exs. We cannot verify those Elixir tests cover all required behaviors.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-named individual functions with docstrings explaining what each verifies (e.g., 'ShapeState has check_fast_loop/1 function', 'Stream clears fast-loop tracking when transitioning to live/up-to-date mode'). The structure is clear and grouped logically.

structured_data_schema.reason: The task involves implementing detection logic in Elixir code, not producing structured data like JSON/CSV/API/DB schemas.

no_typos.reason: All filenames, paths, commands, variable names, and function names in the instruction and tests are consistent and correctly spelled.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: The instruction explicitly names the exact files to modify (shape_state.ex, stream.ex), exact function names, exact struct fields, exact module attributes with values, and exact call patterns. This goes well beyond identifying the domain — it provides the complete implementation blueprint.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: This is a code-only task, not a config-edit task.
